skill.name: word-read-write
skill.description: Create and read Microsoft Word (.docx) documents. Use this skill when you need to generate reports/letters/templates as .docx or extract readable text from existing .docx files.
skill.summary: Create, read, and validate Microsoft Word .docx documents programmatically using Node.js (docx library) and Python scripts for unpacking, editing, and repacking Office Open XML files.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: The skill provides DOCX document creation, unpacking, editing, and validation tools. Static analysis flagged 770 potential issues, but the vast majority are false positives from OOXML schema definition files (XSD) and legitimate document processing patterns. The main real risks are subprocess execution of LibreOffice (soffice) and GCC for document processing, and zip archive extraction operations that carry zip slip risk. No malicious intent, credential exfiltration, or C2 behavior was found. All detected patterns serve the legitimate purpose of Office Open XML document manipulation.

high_findings.title: Subprocess Execution of External Binaries
high_findings.description: The skill executes soffice (LibreOffice) and gcc via subprocess.run() for DOCX processing. Arguments are fixed and not directly user-controlled, but external binary execution represents a real risk in sandboxed environments.

high_findings.title: Archive Extraction Without Path Sanitization (Zip Slip Risk)
high_findings.description: zipfile.ZipFile.extractall() is used in multiple scripts without explicit path traversal validation. While the extracted files are user-supplied Office documents, a crafted malicious archive could write files outside the target directory.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Operations on Temp Directories
medium_findings.description: Multiple scripts create and manipulate files in system temp directories (tempfile.TemporaryDirectory, tempfile.mkdtemp). This is expected for document processing but could leave artifacts if exceptions occur.

medium_findings.title: Hidden File Access in Validation
medium_findings.description: The validate.py script accesses hidden files (dotfiles) when scanning directory contents. These are legitimate XML schema files but indicate broad filesystem scanning that could access sensitive hidden files in other contexts.

medium_findings.title: Environment Variable Copy for Subprocess
medium_findings.description: get_soffice_env() copies the entire current environment for subprocess calls. While it only sets SAL_USE_VCLPLUGIN and optionally LD_PRELOAD, passing the full environment could expose secrets to child processes.

low_findings.title: Smart Quote Unicode Escape Sequences Flagged as Obfuscation
low_findings.description: Unicode escape sequences in comment.py and unpack.py map smart quote characters to XML entities. These are legitimate character-to-entity mappings required for OOXML compatibility, not obfuscation.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded OOXML Namespace URIs in XSD Files
low_findings.description: Hundreds of hardcoded URLs in XSD schema files and Python namespace dictionaries are standard OOXML namespace identifiers (schemas.openxmlformats.org, schemas.microsoft.com). These are static constants, not network requests.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection (False Positive)
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged hex ID generators and OOXML attribute values as 'weak cryptographic algorithm' usage. These are random hex IDs for XML paraId attributes and standard OOXML schema identifiers, not cryptographic operations.

low_findings.title: OOXML Schema XSD Files Flagged as C2/Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged standard OOXML schema namespace declarations (containing '..' in URIs and Microsoft domains) as path traversal and C2 keywords. These are legitimate W3C XML schema definitions for Office document validation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Zip Extract Without Path Validation
dangerous_patterns.description: zipfile.ZipFile.extractall() extracts all archive contents without checking for path traversal sequences. A malicious archive with entries like '../../evil.txt' could write outside the target directory.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dynamic C Code Compilation at Runtime
dangerous_patterns.description: scripts/office/soffice.py compiles a C shared library shim at runtime using gcc to work around AF_UNIX socket restrictions. This writes C source to temp directory, compiles it with gcc, and loads it via LD_PRELOAD.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Execution in SKILL.md Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md documents shell commands for npm install, node execution, and pandoc conversion. These are example usage patterns for the skill, not automatically executed, but documented as user-facing instructions.

content.user_title: Create and Edit Microsoft Word Documents
content.value_statement: Creating valid .docx files from scratch requires deep knowledge of OOXML standards, which is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill provides complete document creation via the docx-js library, along with Python tools for unpacking, editing, validating, and repacking Office Open XML files with proper schema conformance.
actual_capabilities: Creates .docx documents with proper page setup, margins, headers, footers, and page numbers
Generates tables with correct column widths, cell shading, borders, and DXA-based sizing
Adds images, bullet lists, numbered lists, table of contents, and heading styles
Extracts text from existing .docx files by converting to Markdown via Pandoc
Unpacks .docx/.pptx/.xlsx files into editable XML and repacks them with validation
limitations: Requires Node.js 18+ and the docx npm package for document creation
Requires LibreOffice (soffice) to accept tracked changes in existing documents
Requires Pandoc CLI tool for text extraction from .docx files
Cannot edit .docx content inline; must unpack to XML, edit, then repack

use_cases.title: Generate Business Reports
use_cases.description: Create formatted .docx reports from structured data with proper tables, headings, headers, footers, and page numbering for professional distribution.
use_cases.target_user: Business professionals who need automated report generation

use_cases.title: Create Document Templates
use_cases.description: Build standardized letter and memo templates with consistent page sizing (US Letter or A4), margins, fonts, and styling that render correctly in Word and Google Docs.
use_cases.target_user: Administrative staff managing organizational document standards

use_cases.title: Validate and Repair Office Files
use_cases.description: Unpack, validate XML structure against OOXML schemas, repair common issues, and repack .docx files to ensure they open without corruption warnings.
use_cases.target_user: Developers working with Office file automation pipelines

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple DOCX Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a .docx report with the title 'Monthly Status Report' as a Heading 1, followed by a bullet list of 3 project updates and a table with 2 columns and 3 rows showing task names and completion percentages.
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate a basic business report from text

prompt_templates.title: Create a DOCX with Tables and Images
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a .docx document with US Letter page size, 1-inch margins, a header that says 'Confidential', a footer with page numbers, a table with 4 columns (Name, Department, Role, Start Date) with header row shading, and an image placeholder.
prompt_templates.scenario: Build a formatted document with advanced layout elements

prompt_templates.title: Unpack and Edit an Existing DOCX
prompt_templates.prompt: Unpack the file 'proposal.docx' to a directory, show me the main document.xml structure, and explain how to add a comment on the first paragraph about revising the budget section.
prompt_templates.scenario: Explore and modify an existing Word document

prompt_templates.title: Validate a Repacked DOCX
prompt_templates.prompt: After editing the unpacked DOCX files in 'output/', validate the document against OOXML schemas check for broken relationships, missing content types, and verify all references are valid before repacking.
prompt_templates.scenario: Ensure document integrity after custom XML edits

output_examples.input: Create a .docx invoice with a table showing 3 line items, a total row, and proper formatting.
output: Source code generated using docx-js library with:
- Section with 1-inch margins and US Letter size
- Bold header row with blue shading (#D5E8F0)
- Total row with a bottom double-border

output_examples.input: Unpack meeting-notes.docx and merge adjacent formatting runs.
output: Smart quotes converted to XML entities
Merged 15 adjacent runs with identical formatting

best_practices: Always set table width, columnWidths, and each cell width using DXA units for consistent rendering across Word and Google Docs
Use HeadingLevel constants for heading styles and include outlineLevel for proper table of contents generation
Use separate Paragraph elements instead of embedding newline characters inside TextRun
anti_patterns: Do not use WidthType.PERCENTAGE for table widths as it can break rendering in Google Docs
Do not insert Unicode bullet characters as plain text; use numbering config with LevelFormat.BULLET instead
Do not modify document.xml without also updating the corresponding .rels relationship files and [Content_Types].xml

faq.question: What dependencies does this skill require?
faq.answer: You need Node.js 18+ with the docx npm package for document creation. For text extraction, install Pandoc. For accepting tracked changes, install LibreOffice.

faq.question: Can I edit an existing DOCX file with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, but indirectly. Use the Python scripts to unpack the DOCX to XML files, edit the XML, then repack. The comment.py script can add Word comments to unpacked files.

faq.question: Does this skill work with PowerPoint or Excel files?
faq.answer: The validation and unpack/repack scripts support PPTX and XLSX files. Document creation via docx-js is specific to DOCX format only.

faq.question: Why does the skill need to run external commands?
faq.answer: The skill runs soffice (LibreOffice) to accept tracked changes and for document comparison. It also compiles a small C shim if Unix sockets are blocked in sandboxed environments.

faq.question: What security risks should I be aware of?
faq.answer: The skill runs external programs (soffice, gcc, pandoc) via subprocess and extracts ZIP archives. Ensure you only process documents from trusted sources to avoid zip slip attacks.

faq.question: Does this skill make network requests?
faq.answer: No. The hardcoded URIs in the code are OOXML namespace identifiers used for XML schema validation, not actual network requests. The skill operates entirely locally.
